General Admission Steps.val: Admission Application Form must be filled online by following the instructions.
Applications received through post or by hand will not be accepted/entertained.
The online system assigns a unique Application Reference Number (ARN) to each applicant, which must be noted for later use.

Applicant will confirm that information entered in the Admission Application Form is correct. In case of incorrect information, the University has all the rights to cancel the application/admission without any fee refund.
Applicant will deposit the Application Fee in the designated Bank. Fee is non-refundable/adjustable.
Upon payment of the Application Processing Fee (and additional Admission Test Fee), the applicant will print his/her ADMIT CARD bearing the applicant’s TEST ROLL NUMBER.
The applicant should then visit the test campus on the specified date and time as mentioned on the Admit card.
Admission of a student automatically stands cancelled if he or she fails in the required exam whose result was not available at the time of admission.

General Admission Notes.val: Admission Test is held at all campuses of the University (Karachi, Lahore, Islamabad, and Peshawar).
GRE (subject test) can apply for admission to graduate programs, i.e. MS, MBA or PhD.
SAT examination can apply for admission to the undergraduate programs, i.e. BBA or BS.
The Institution code for receiving SAT scores is 4575.
The Institution code for receiving GRE scores is 7386.
Foreign nationals must submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the Ministry of Education, Government of Pakistan at the time of admission.

Rs. 3000 is payable by all applicants including those who are seeking admission on the basis of NTS (NAT or GAT) test. If an applicant wants to take more than one Admission Test, then an Additional Fee of Rs. 500 is payable for each additional Admission Test.
Admission policy is merit‑based with no quotas or donation seats.
Foreign nationals need NOC from Ministry of Federal Education and Professional Training.
Provisional admission allowed for candidates awaiting FA/FSc/A‑level results.
Maximum time allowed to complete degree requirements: BS/BBA 6 years, MBA 3 years, MS 4 years, PhD 8 years (as per HEC guidelines).
Double admission (concurrent admission in more than one degree) is not allowed.
Suspension of registration can occur due to disciplinary action, unpaid dues, or failure to complete registration formalities.
Readmission rules: former students may be readmitted to graduate or undergraduate programs after passing an admission test, subject to conditions on prior warnings and credit transfer.

Transfer of students between campuses is permitted only on grounds such as death of a parent, posting of a parent, or marriage of a female student, and not allowed if the student has disciplinary or financial issues.
Semester freeze is allowed for exceptional circumstances with a fee; first two semesters for BS and first semester for MS cannot be frozen.

Scholarship_notes.val: Merit scholarship awarded to top three position holders of each Examination Board and top three in university merit list; covers full tuition for four years, continuation requires GPA >= 3.0.
HEC scholarships for MS and PhD cover tuition, stipend and book allowance.
Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) offers 42 scholarships to indigent Punjab domicile students for 4-year undergraduate studies, partial tuition covered.
Sindh Government Endowment Board scholarships for Karachi campus, need‑cum‑merit, full tuition, 25 new scholarships per year, 60% rural quota.
Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Education Foundation scholarship for Peshawar campus, need‑cum‑merit, 80% tuition, gender‑balanced.
Baluchistan Government Endowment Board scholarship for Karachi campus, full tuition, renewable annually.
OSAFA (Old Students Association of FAST) provides interest‑free study loans for indigent students; loan covers tuition only and is renewable each semester.
Various community scholarships (Memon, Bohra, INFAQ, Agha Khan, Hindu, etc.) and alumni assistance are available on specific campuses.
Assistantships are offered to students for campus activities with compensation.
Bank loans from PICIC, NIB, Alfalah, Islami and MCB are available.
Study loan repayment starts three months after graduation, to be completed within four years; employers may deduct repayments at source.

research_and_faculty_notes.val: PhD programs require 6 coursework credits (18 credit hours), comprehensive examination with minimum CGPA 3.0 within two years, and at least one published research paper in an HEC‑approved journal before thesis submission.

Eligibility for financial grants: open merit admission, minimum CGPA 2.0 for continuation, no disciplinary action, one grant per student.

facilities_notes.val: Hostel Accommodation is limited and is provided on the basis of merit; local students are not eligible to apply. Selectees may apply to Senior Warden, University of Engineering and Technology, Lahore for hostel accommodation; online on the admission portal after confirmation of admission and deposit the hostel fee on the challan form downloadable from the admission portal and provide a copy to dues section.

Female candidates selected for admission at New Campus (Kala Shah Kaku) should apply to the Senior Warden, University of Engineering and Technology, Lahore for hostel accommodation. For hostel accommodation at New Campus, Faisalabad Campus, Narowal Campus, and Rachna College of Engineering and Technology, Gujranwala, the selectees should apply to the Warden Office of the concerning campus.
The sports complex includes an Olympic-size swimming pool, tennis and squash courts, a gymnasium and an impressive stadium.

admission_requirement_for_new_student.val: Minimum score requirement: 50% for all programs. Eligibility for Computer Engineering program: 60% minimum score.

General Admission Steps.val: Apply online via the admission portal.
Submit completed application form and required supporting documents.
Pay the application processing fee of Rs. 3,500.
Tuition fees are collected on a semester basis; 5% administrative charge applies if paying semester‑wise, waived for lump‑sum payment for two semesters.
Late payment incurs a fine of Rs 50 per day.

General Admission Notes.val: Undergraduate admissions contact: ugadmissions@giki.edu.pk, phone 0938-281026, extensions 2301,2342,2354.
Graduate admissions contact: gadmissions@giki.edu.pk, phone 0938-281026, extension 2450.

Undergraduate admission important dates: Application Start 15-Mar-2026, Application Deadline 15-May-2026, Admission Test 6–10 July 2026, Merit List 21 July 2026, Joining & Orientation 29-30 Aug 2026, Commencement of Classes 31 Aug 2026.
Graduate admission important dates: Application Deadline 03-Jul-2026, Admission Test 23-Jul-2026, Interviews 28-29-Jul-2026, Session Starts 31-Aug-2026.
Admission to Bachelor programs is based on earlier educational achievements and score in admission test covering Physics and Mathematics; no quotas, no age limit, open to all genders and religions.
Graduate admission requires passing GRE (General) or HEC-ETC HAT Test, or GIKI Admission Test; GRE/HAT exempt from GIKI test.
The institute operates on a semester system with continuous evaluation; minimum GPA 2.00 to remain in good standing; probation if GPA falls below 2.00.

Scholarship_notes.val: GIK Merit Scholarship: Fully funded for top merit candidates (Engineering 18, MGS 2).
Financial Assistance by GIK Institute: Need cum merit basis, 40 scholarships, fully or partially funded.
Financial Assistance for Top Female: Fully funded for top female merit candidates (Engineering 7, MGS 3).
Punjab Educational Endowment Fund (PEEF) Scholarship: 10 seats, partially covered, eligibility: Punjab domicile, family income < Rs. 720,000.
Chief Minister Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Scholarship: 20 seats, fully funded, KP domicile, family income < Rs. 600,000.
GIK Alumni Association Scholarship: 55 seats, fully/partially funded for needy students from 2nd year onward.
Ihsan Trust loan: 20 partial funded loans for needy students after admission offer.
Sindh Scholarship: 5 fully funded seats for Sindh domicile, family income < Rs. 1,200,000, after joining.
Chief Minister Educational Endowment Fund Scholarship (CMEEF): 4 fully funded seats for KP domicile, family income < Rs. 1,200,000, after joining.
Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Education Foundation Scholarship: 2 fully funded seats, family income < Rs. 1,920,000, after joining.
Fast Cables Scholarship: 1 fully funded seat for economically disadvantaged areas, after joining.
HBL Platinum Scholarship: 1 partially funded seat, need-cum-merit basis, after joining.
ICI Scholarship: 1 fully funded seat for top female candidates, after joining.
Lucky Cement Scholarship: 1 fully funded seat for Pakistani nationals, preference to KP domicile, after joining.
Bestway Educational Foundation scholarship: 1 partially funded seat, merit-cum-need basis, after joining.
Abdullah Foundation scholarship: 4 fully funded seats, merit-cum-need basis, after joining.
BARD Foundation: 1 fully funded seat for rural area students meeting GIK financial assistance criteria, after joining.
PEL Scholarship: 1 fully funded seat for Electrical Engineering students meeting GIK FA criteria, after joining.
Additional financial assistance processing fee of Rs. 1,000 required in addition to the Rs. 3,500 admission processing fee.
Financial assistance loans are repayable in six monthly installments starting one year after graduation.

notes.val: Information such as program duration, tuition fees, and detailed admission requirements were not provided in the source markdown.
Civil Engineering department offers BS, MS, and PhD programs with streams including Structural, Transportation, Water Resources, Environmental, Geotechnical, Geomatics, and Construction Management.
The institute is fully residential, co‑educational, and provides counseling services through the Dean of Student Affairs and a wellness counselor.
Academic policies include a 4‑year normal duration (maximum 6 years), GPA requirement of 2.00, probation rules, and a credit system totaling 135/136 credit hours.
